mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0%, labor market slack dissipates, wage pressures emerge, and corporate earnings growth accelerates, driving positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 8.0% in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong manufacturing output growth combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates synchronized demand recovery, which expands profit margins and lifts equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary regime, rising short-term rates typically compress valuations, but if industrial production simultaneously expands, earnings resilience offsets rate sensitivity, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises greater than 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in greater than 60% of triggered instances despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces recession risk premiums and supports corporate cash flow expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 shows sequential growth, failing a one-sided t-test at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 215.0, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting market repricing of discount rates and causing negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses above 215.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls coupled with declining housing starts signal a rotation from construction to services, which historically precedes equity market rallies over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or zero directional accuracy over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, consumer credit expansion directly fuels discretionary spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive 20-day equity returns despite weak labor metrics.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 9.0% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative when RSAFS rises while UNRATE stays above 9.0% in more than 50% of observations.
